content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 207 • SACRAMENTO, 95814 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 15, 1978

Barbara Berkley
Commission on California State
Government Organization and
Economy
11th & L Building, Suite 550
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Berkley:

This letter is in response to your letter of October 20,
1978, concerning the reportability of leasehold interests
under your agency's conflict of interest code.

First, on behalf of Commissioner Post, you asked whether
a five year lease with a ten year option it reportable. We
think that the official's leasehold interest includes both
the lease and any option to renew the lease. Therefore, in
determining whether the interest will expire within ten
years, one must add the period for which the official has
the option to renew the lease to the years remaining on the
lease.

Your second question concerns circumstances under which
a leasehold interest need not be reported. Simply stated, a
leasehold interest is not reportable unless it will extend for
at least ten more years and has a value of $1,000 or more.
If it fails to satisfy either of these criteria, it is not
reportable. Therefore, I believe the exemption is more use-
ful than Commissioner Post had interpreted it to be.

If you have any further questions, please do not not
hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim, Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

TP:cjb

STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

COMMISSION ON CALIFORNIA STATE GOVERNMENT ORGANIZATION AND ECONOMY
11th & L Building, Suite 550, (916) 445-2125
Sacramento 95814

CHAIRMAN
NATHAN SHAPELL
Beverly Hills

VICE-CHAIRMAN
DONALD G. LIVINGSTON
Los Angeles

ALFRED E. ALQUIST
Senator, San Jose

MAURICE RENE CHEZ
Los Angeles

DAVID P. DAWSON
Assemblyman, Modesto

JACK H. FENTON
Assemblyman, Healdsburg

EDWARD M. FRYER
Portola Valley

RICHARD D. HAYDEN
Assemblyman, Cupertino

NANCIE R. KNAPP
Los Angeles

MILTON MARKS
Senator, San Francisco

MANNING J. POST
Los Angeles

LLOYD BIGLER
Los Angeles

JEAN KINDY WALKER
Modesto

L.H. HAILCOMB
Executive Director


                                        October 20, 1978


Ted Prin, Chief
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA    95814

Dear Ted:

    A few days ago I received a FPPC informational dated October 10 which
I duplicated and sent to all public members.

    Commissioner Post has a question concerning the section on leaseholds
(Page 2):

            If you have a five year lease with a
            10 year option, does this constitute
            a 15 year lease?

    He also expressed his dismay at the example of exemption which was
cited; namely, no leasehold of less than 10 years or with a value of
$1,000 needed to be reported. He wanted you to know that this, in
fact, constitutes no exemption at all in the area of leaseholds.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        BARBARA BERKLEY
                                        Assistant to
                                        Executive Director

Enclosure

NEW LEGISLATION

The Legislature recently amended Government Code Section 87302
to provide that persons newly appointed to an elective or non-
civil service position (effective January 1, 1979) should file
Statement of Economic Interests within 10 days after assuming
office. Previously the law required that Statements be filed
at least 10 days prior to assuming office.

ATTORNEY GENERAL'S OPINION

In response to a request from the Fair Political Practices Commission
the Attorney General issued an opinion (NO. CV 78/12) which provides
that cities may not pay fines imposed upon city councilmembers when
a Statement is filed late. The Attorney General concluded that to
permit a public official to escape personal responsibility for
filing would be contrary to the intent of the Political Reform Act.
Based on this opinion we think it would be unlawful for any agency
to pay the late fines of its members or employees.

LEASEHOLDS

A lease on real property which will not expire for at least ten years
after the first day of the period covered by the filer's Statement
of Economic Interest is reportable if the value of the leasehold
interest is more than $1,000. The value of the leasehold interest
is the total amount of rent owed by the filer during the period covered
by the Statement. When reporting a leasehold interest the filer
must include the number of years remaining on the lease, the address
or other precise location of the leased property and the date upon
which the lease became effective or terminate if either date occurred
during the period covered by the Statement. No leasehold of less
than 10 years or with a value of $1,000 or less need be reported.
(FPPC Regulation 18233)

TRUSTS

Filers must report any interests in real property, sources of income
and investments of a trust in which the filer, spouse or dependent
children individually or cumulatively have a direct, indirect or
beneficial interest of 10% or greater.

According to FPPC Regulation 18234, a filer has an interest in a
trust if the filer is:

1. A maker and:

(a) can revoke or terminate the trust;

(b) has reserved any rights to the income or principal of
the trust or retained any reversionary or remainder
interest; or
